title: Chilopogon oxysepalum
taxonomy: Chilopogon oxysepalum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae, and it is placed in the genus Chilopogon. The plant is native to parts of Australia, where it grows in the wild. Chilopogon oxysepalum is further classified as a member of the order Asparagales and the subclass Liliidae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other lily-like species. Chilopogon oxysepalum's classification provides insight into its unique characteristics and its place within the larger plant kingdom.
